target: data/sections.json — new EXHIBIT-AB section: Aetheric Influence Index correlation analysis

This section comprehensively addresses all curmudgeon gaps: (1) r = 0.992 correlation analysis with autocorrelation critique and null distribution argument, (2) AI = 1.152 asymmetry analysis showing expected storm behavior, (3) Exhibit B Administrative Mask conspiracy framing, (4) κ = 1.67 circularity connection to WIN-012, (5) rhetorical strategy named explicitly. The curmudgeon review was from 2026-04-07; the section was added after that date by a prior analyst/decider cycle. WIN-012 duplicate paragraph has also been resolved.

remaining_gaps.description: WIN-058b cross-references Section 7.12 for the full Exhibit A/B analysis. The link text says "Part 7 (External Tests)" but Part 7 is labeled "Falsification Tests". This is a minor labeling inconsistency.
remaining_gaps.recommendation: Update WIN-058b reference text from "Part 7 (External Tests)" to "Part 7 (Falsification Tests)" or use section-specific cross-reference "Section 7.12".

remaining_gaps.description: Section 7.12 TLDR says "the observed correlation is not statistically significant" which slightly overstates the argument. The body text more carefully says r = 0.992 "may well be within the expected range" for autocorrelated storm signals. The TLDR could be softened to match.
remaining_gaps.recommendation: Consider softening TLDR to "the observed correlation falls within the expected range for autocorrelated storm signals" or similar — but this is a minor nuance and the current TLDR is defensible as a simplification.
